w:r.w:t: University is a public university founded in March 2005, (Council of Ministers, Regulations No. 236/2011) as an autonomous higher education institution under the management of the Ministry of Education, located at Bale-Robe Town,
Regional State, Ethiopia. It currently has enrolled around 24,961 students in undergraduate and postgraduate programs hosted by 79 departments which are available under its
colleges and Three schools. The colleges in

University are: College of Business and Economics, College of Social Sciences and Humanities, College of Natural and Computational Sciences, College of Agriculture and Natural Resources, College of Education and Behavioral Sciences and College of Engineering, College of Computing. In addition, the three schools are, School of Health Sciences, School of
University has three campuses, main campus is located at Bale Robe,
University has three main integrated research centers located at Robe main University campus,
niversity is thriving with a vision of becoming one of the top five “societal problem-solving” universities in Ethiopia
, with a Motto of “Excellence through Diversity”.
Becoming one of the top five societal problem-solving univ

The mission of MWU is to produce competent entrepreneurial as well as employable graduates, provide need-based community services and problem-solving research outputs through relevant and quality education, research, training and consultancy service to foster socio-economic development in the country.
Research and Development Focus areas and thematic areas of
Comprehensive Crop Production and Productivity Advancement
Modernizations of Farm implements and efficient farming
Emerging and re-emerging infectious diseases, and chronic
Clinical practice, mental health, neurologic disorders, maternal, and child, Life Course Health, and nutrition
Medical tourism and volunteer activities/mobility and broad area of medical supports
Synergies for Sustainable Tourism and National
Tourism Economics, Ecotourism and Local Development
Sustaining the Bale Mountain National Park and Other Tourism Potentials in the University Catchment Area
Social Dynamism, Heritage Management and Conservation, and Human Rights, Environment, and
Indigenous Knowledge Systems for enhancing education, food security, Environmental protection and Conserving
Sustainable Bale Mountain National Park Management
Biodiversity Conservation, Integrated Natural Resource and Environmental Management
Conservation of Bale Mountains Ecological Region
Natural Resources, and conflict resolution and health care systems
Green Technologies, Smart and sustainable Infrastructure
Intelligent, AI and Secure Systems, and building and road infrastructure for Human, social
Applications of AI, Big Data and Data Science in Education, Health,
Biostatistics, Bioinformatics, and Machine Learning for Agriculture and
Social media, internet security and social wellbeing
in Physics (specialization in Solid state Physics)
MA in Urban Planning and Management
in GIS, Land Use Planning & Management
MA in A/ Oromo and Literature
MA in Applied Linguistics in Teaching Amharic
MA in Curriculum & Teachers Professional Development
Post Graduate Programs to be launched

The area where MWU is situated is rich in tourist destination, one of the 36 global biodiversity hotspot areas, and higher agricultural production and harvesting twice a year paves the way for the university to focus on:
in Ethiopia, is named after the
. According to the Oromo oral traditions, ‘
’ is the birth place of the
. It is believed that the center of the spiritual, cultural and political systems where the Oromo’s council of assembly (
) was taking place since the beginning of
System. This place is located at the south eastern part of the Ethiopian highlands around the upper course of the
River, in southernmost tip of Bale Zone (in the district of
) at the distance of 640 km from Addis Ababa, the capital city of the country, Ethiopia and 227 km from
the Robe town, where the main campus of MWU is found. The foundation stone of
the Bale Zonal town of Robe. O
University has been highly dedicated to its mission and vision to produce capable graduates to today’s market.
University is located in the southeastern part of our country
main campus in Bale Robe town.
The University considers itself advantageous with regard to
which has significant importance for the countries
he biodiversity, natural resource and ecosystem services of the Bale highlands
wealth and it is one of the
Not to mention the abundant tourist attraction sites of Bale which will be the countries future
tourism destination. Moreover, considering the health services that
Hospital currently providing to Southeastern part of the country, and the completion of
building of the hospital will definitely make the University not only academic center but also medical hub of the region.
In addition to the location significance
of the area, the human capital as well as the academic departments, the UG and PG programs are also mainly oriented to the University’s Center of Excellence.
aking this into account, MWU decided to have four center of excellence namely: Agriculture, Tourism, Biodiversity and Health Science.
The university also launched four mega research projects on 2014 EC related to the center of excellences
and the researches are producing tangible outcomes
Here below briefly indicated why these four center of excellences are
: Partial view of location of Universities in Ethiopia and Catchment area of
The highland part of Bale and
area have a temperate climate favorable for crop and livestock production. The soils in the region are generally fertile, and the rainfall is sufficient for crop
production. However, the lowland region is also prone to drought, which can have a devastating impact on agricultural production.
In recent years, there has been a growing emphasis on agricultural development in Bale and

. The government has invested in irrigation projects, and there has been a focus on introducing improved crop varieties and livestock breeds. These interventions have helped to improve agricultural productivity in the region, and have contributed to food security.
significance of the area for food production but also there are numerous agricultural research centers and ATVET colleges in Bale and
areas. Hence, the University as larger institute is working to mobilize stakeholders to work together on common agendas.
In line with the government policy direction of improving agricultural production and productivity,
University has done tremendous community services and research project mainly
catchment area of intervention. In the last five years

and the local farmers to apply cluster wheat farming and supported improved seeds and fertilizers. For example, in the current crop year, MWU have supported farmers who are organized in clusters for wheat farming in eight

Recently the government announced a plan to attain wheat self-sufficiency by 2022 in order to reduce the Nation’s foreign currency expenditure in importing large amount of wheat. This ambitious plan can be achieved by in
creasing wheat productivity in the rain-fed agro-ecologies and expansion of production to the irrigable lowland areas.
Wheat production and productivity can be increased through a concerted effort of various national and international parties in which
University, as a dominant academic and research institution in the southeastern Ethiopia, is expected to play a major role because it is geographically located in the

The goal of this project is to increase bread wheat production and productivity in southeastern Ethiopia in an integrated approach to complement the effort of the government of Ethiopia to attaining wheat self-sufficiency.
This project is basically intended to benefit the communities which reside in Bale, East Bale and West
Zones, especially, farmers, and agro-pastoral and pastoral communities.
Moreover, the University has three Integrated Research Centers at Robe main campus,
centers. Multiple, PVS and FTC activities are being undertaken at these

University have start numerous initiatives to revitalizing the research center for enhanced community services and agricultural research undertakings. With this brief points being indicated to show our Universities vision to become one of the leading Agricultural research center in the country.

w:r.w:t: University is surrounded by many beautiful Parks, Shrines,
and historical areas which catch the attention of people from various parts of the world. That is why the University considered Tourism as one of its center of excellence.
University community is also happy to know that the
sustainable management of the park and the overall ecosystem of area.

University, or wants to conduct research on any topic which is pertinent to the university, or Bale Zone will find their stay most enjoyable. Out of the four mega research projects launched, Tourism and Heritage Management related mega research project is launched in 2014 EC. The project have produced imminent documents which are vital to enhance tourist destination sites, Shri
nes and historical site in Bale,
In addition, in view to improve the hotel availability for tourist and other travelers in Bale,
town which determined to enhance tourist flow to Bale and surrounding areas.
Major tourist attraction site in the Bale areas are briefly highlighted below:

is a UNESCO World Heritage Site located in the Bale Mountains of Ethiopia. It is the largest Afro-alpine habitat park in Africa, and is home to a diverse range of wildlife, including the Ethiopian wolf, the giant mole rat,
. The park is also known for its stunning scenery, including its high mountains, sweeping valleys, and dramatic escarpment. The park due to abundant diversity is said to be “many world in one park”.

Tourism in the Bale Mountains is growing, and there are a number of tour operators that offer guided hikes, camping trips, and bird watching excursions. The park is also a popular destination for mountain climbers, who come to challenge themselves on the peaks of
There are a number of hiking trails in the park, ranging from easy to challenging. The
Plateau is a popular destination for hikers, as it offers stunning views of the surrounding mountains.
The Park is crucial to the survival of the Mountain
and the Ethiopian Wolf (Red Fox). The park gives the chance of viewing over 46 mammals and more than 260 bird species out of which 16 are endemic to Ethiopia.
Due to the vast and diverse wild life and vegetation dynamics the park is known as
Different View of Bale Mountains National Park
is the historical, cultural, belief and political center for Oromo people. It is
away from the university in the south-west direction
. It covers an area of about 200 sq. km. There is yet a spectacular stream in
area which is again surrounded by 11Oda trees around the stream. The Oromo people used to sit under the shade of these trees whenever they had meetings. Out of the five main
is one the famous heritage sites. As the university is named after this unique place, much is expected from the university to study and promote about it.
This is the site of a fantastic complex of natural caves, dissociated by the
River, as it finds its way into the nearby mountains. This cave is at about 110kms away from Robe town (where
University is found) towards the eastern direction.

is a unique cave with flat geography and deep gorge where pilgrimage celebrants visit it in the months of November and December each year. Besides, there is a very deep hole nearby created naturally, which some people call it to be “a hole which was used for mixing mud while the earth was formed”. Furthermore, it is an area where different flora and fauna adapt themselves to low and

Dire Sheik Hussein is found 180kms to the northeast of Robe town. This historical place is visited at least two times a year by people from different angles of the country as well as abroad. People, visit this place for various purposes like religious ceremonies, for relaxation, for research and other purposes. Because of the historical and artistic nature of the place, it attracts visitors from different areas and makes them exchange cultures, values, experiences and beliefs.
Bale Eco-Region (BER), among the 36 Global Biodiversity Hotspots provides several ecosystem services locally, regionally and globally. The

is not only known for its wealth of biodiversity and immense natural resources but also its ecological and hydrological significance, which supports livelihoods of more than 20 million people in Southeastern Ethiopia, Somalia and part of Northeastern Kenya. More than 40 streams that flow to Southern and Southeastern part of our country, Somalia and Kenya originates from the Bale highlands
, hence the area is considered to be main water sources area
, due its significance and complex relationships with the beneficiaries in the two major basins, ecosystem service study has become an important subject of research in recent years.
mountain area have attracted the attention of
responsible for sustainable management of ecosystem services.
University has been working with many stakeholders to preserve and sustainably manage natural resources and biodiversity of the Bale
natural resources conservation and mountain rehabilitation” mega research project launched in 2014 EC by the shows the commitments of
MWU have also won a competitive bid to prepare

Planning work with Ministry of Water and Energy. The project has higher regional and national significance aimed at improving sustainable water resources development and management of the river basin. T
o support the biodiversity and natural resources conservation works in the fragile and mountainous regions of the area
natural resources conservation Mega project in 2014 EC”. The project has shown tangible outcomes and the area has started to regenerate vegetation since the inception of the mega project.
Here below indicated a glimpse of wild life endemic animals and biodiversity of Bale Mountains:

Bale Mountains offer some fine high- altitude and foot trekking, and the streams of the park- which become important rivers further downstream are well stocked with rainbow and brown trout. In addition to this, there are other attractive areas like
Natural forest which is the richest forest in natural coffee. Not only has this Bale Mountain lodged which placed in
Forest at very attractive sites and international level with every accommodation.
An Endemic Red Fox / Ethiopian Wolf
There are certain geological features that remain an enigma to geologists and glaciologists such as the
that appear on shallow hillsides on the
. Boulder grooves (large stone sorted stripes two to four meters wide and eighty meters long), till ridges and numerous glacial valleys, such as the
Valley on the northeast facing slopes of the
, provide evidence of the ice-age effects on the landscape of BMNP. Until the beginning of
(13,000 to 14,000 years ago) the snowline was at 3,700 meters and the upper tree limit in the

was well below 3,000 meters. Fluctuations in climate over the last historical period, including the last 3,000 years, have dramatically affected the vegetation and other biodiversity in the highlands.

forest is the second largest stand of moist tropical forest remaining in Ethiopia. Approximately half of the park falls in this forest which ranges from 3500m to 1,500m a slat the southern park boundary. This forest is home to high levels of biodiversity and endemism, including many threatened species such as

. Lions and endangered African wild dogs are still found in this forest, as are giant forest hogs. However its floral and faunal composition is still poorly documented. The forest is also rich in wild coffee. The other great chance is Bale Mountain Lodge is also located in the middle of this forest. The lodge is found
in a very fascinating and attractive site and it is an international lodge which delivery overall services.
Harenna Forest , the Richest Forest in Natural Coffee
largest public institute in the Southeastern part of Ethiopia, no other universities established in this direction up to
frontier. Therefore, the university has huge responsibility to serve the local community including in the remotest part
Moreover, considering the health services that
Referral Hospital currently providing to Southeastern part of the country, and the completion of the eight

building of the hospital will definitely make the University not only academic center but also medical hub of the region. In addition to the health and medical services that the University provide at the GRH, high profile university surgeon provides free medical/surgical service to remote community occasionally such

scan and MRI and more. As you can imagine equipping such huge Hospital will not be possible by government budget only hence University president established center committee to mobilize medical equipment
though different organizations of which Rotary is huge potential.
completion of this hospital will certainly boost the University’s capacity to provide
institution that provides exceptionally high concentrations of expertise and related resources focused on particular medical areas
nd deliver in a comprehensive and
dedicated to advancing the knowledge and practice of
health science and medicine, and which also
play a vital role in improving the health of individuals and populations
in the South eastern parts of our country.
Referral Hospital building in completion stage
